intro: JavaScript manages dynamic application lifecycles inside a single-threaded web or server event loop. Shifting to C replaces this abstraction with direct hardware access, absolute structural memory management, and compiled machine code. Developers undertake this fundamental shift to write low-level systems, high-throughput engines, or hardware driver extensions where high runtime overhead cannot be tolerated.

diffs.to: Static, weakly typed, structural manifest declaration requirements.

diffs.from: Automatic tracking handled by engine garbage collection.
diffs.to: Manual allocation and memory cleanup via system pointers.

diffs.from: Just-In-Time (JIT) bytecode interpretation inside V8 or similar engines.
diffs.to: Ahead-Of-Time (AOT) direct binary generation via compilers.

diffs.from: Single-threaded event loop utilizing non-blocking asynchronous callbacks.
diffs.to: Multi-threaded execution pathways managed natively via OS threads.

diffs.from: Dynamic structural objects and flexible array buffers.
diffs.to: Fixed structures (structs) and rigid, fixed-size memory arrays.

examples.title: Memory Struct Allocations
examples.description: Allocating storage data layouts explicitly across functional boundaries.

examples.title: Array Creation and Loop
examples.description: Instantiating collections and applying incremental traversal rules.

tips.title: Prevent Memory Leaks
tips.body: Track every malloc allocation across execution branches and ensure free() is called explicitly.

tips.title: Track Array Sizes Natively
tips.body: Pass collection sizes alongside pointers to functions, because C arrays do not expose a length property.

tips.title: Avoid String Overflows
tips.body: Replace open string assignments with strncpy to prevent data corruption beyond buffer boundaries.

tips.title: Inspect Pointer Addresses
tips.body: Use reference symbols (&) and dereference symbols (*) carefully to update memory safely without segmentation faults.
